Requirement.Name: Exploration State Machine
Requirement.Content: The system shall implement an exploration state machine with, at minimum, the following states: IDLE, EXPLORE, RETURN_TO_START, and SECOND_RUN, and shall transition between these states based on exploration progress and receipt of explicit PATH_COMPLETED or PATH_ABORTED status signals or path execution timeout handling as defined by the path execution completion, abort, and timeout conditions.

Requirement.Name: State Machine Transitions
Requirement.Content: The system shall implement the following state transitions based on path execution status signals and timeouts: from IDLE to EXPLORE when the exploration start condition is met; from EXPLORE to RETURN_TO_START when exploration is marked as done; from RETURN_TO_START to SECOND_RUN when a PATH_COMPLETED status for the return path is received and no PATH_ABORTED status or PATH_TIMEOUT status has been raised; from SECOND_RUN to IDLE when a PATH_COMPLETED status for the second-run path is received; and from RETURN_TO_START or SECOND_RUN back to IDLE when a PATH_ABORTED status or PATH_TIMEOUT status is raised or a severe fault causes a safe stop that is followed by a system reset.

Requirement.Name: Exploration Start Condition
Requirement.Content: The system shall enter the EXPLORE state from the IDLE state when the robot is powered on, initialized, and a start signal is received from a predefined trigger such as a user button press or software command.

Requirement.Name: Exploration Start Time Definition
Requirement.Content: The system shall define the exploration start time as the instant when the transition from IDLE to EXPLORE is performed according to the exploration start condition.

Requirement.Name: Unknown Cell Selection
Requirement.Content: While in the EXPLORE state, the system shall periodically select a target cell that is currently marked as UNKNOWN in the internal maze representation and is reachable from the robot's current position according to the defined reachability criteria.

Requirement.Name: Exploration Path Planning
Requirement.Content: For each selected UNKNOWN target cell, the system shall compute a collision-free path from the robot's current grid cell to the target grid cell using the current internal maze representation and the defined reachability and connectivity rules.

Requirement.Name: Exploration Termination Condition
Requirement.Content: The system shall terminate the EXPLORE state and mark exploration as done when either (a) no UNKNOWN cells are reachable from the robot's current position according to the defined reachability criteria or (b) the 5-minute exploration time budget defined for mapping is reached.

Requirement.Name: Continuous Wall Avoidance During Exploration
Requirement.Content: While in the EXPLORE state, the system shall continuously adjust its motion commands such that the robot remains within a configured safety distance from any detected wall, overriding planned motion commands when necessary to avoid collision.

Requirement.Name: Wall Detection From Sensors
Requirement.Content: The system shall interpret distance or proximity readings from wall-related sensors to detect the presence of walls within a configurable detection range in front of and to the sides of the robot.

Requirement.Name: Wall Proximity Thresholds
Requirement.Content: The system shall maintain at least two configurable distance thresholds for each wall sensor direction: a warning distance and a hard-stop distance, and shall treat readings below these thresholds as proximity and collision risk respectively.

Requirement.Name: Reactive Obstacle Avoidance
Requirement.Content: When a wall is detected within the warning distance, the system shall modify the current motion commands to steer away from the wall, and when a wall is detected within the hard-stop distance, the system shall immediately command the actuators to stop forward motion.

Requirement.Name: Wall Detection Update Rate
Requirement.Content: The system shall perform wall detection and avoidance decision updates on the same periodic schedule as the general sensor processing loop, at a nominal rate within the configured sensor processing range and with the jitter bounds defined for sensor processing.

Requirement.Name: Maze Grid Representation
Requirement.Content: The system shall represent the maze as a two-dimensional grid of discrete cells, where each cell stores at least its classification state FREE, BLOCKED, or UNKNOWN, and its occupancy update status, and shall index cells according to the defined row and column indexing convention.

Requirement.Name: Maze Maximum Dimensions
Requirement.Content: The internal grid representation shall be capable of representing mazes of at least 16 cells by 16 cells, and for sizing and performance purposes the system shall assume a design point of up to 16 cells by 16 cells, while support for larger mazes is permitted but not covered by performance guarantees.

Requirement.Name: Grid Cell Resolution
Requirement.Content: The system shall define a fixed physical size for each grid cell and shall map physical distances to grid coordinates such that each cell corresponds to a square area of 18 centimeters by 18 centimeters with a tolerance of plus or minus 1 centimeter.

Requirement.Name: Grid Cell Size Calibration
Requirement.Content: The system shall provide a calibration or configuration procedure that measures the effective physical distance traveled for a known number of commanded cell movements and adjusts mapping parameters so that the resulting effective cell size lies within the specified tolerance, and shall report a calibration failure if this cannot be achieved.

Requirement.Name: Grid Indexing Convention
Requirement.Content: The system shall index each grid cell by a pair of integer indices (row, column) where the row index increases in the positive y-direction and the column index increases in the positive x-direction of the map frame, and shall treat the row index as the primary key and the column index as the secondary key for lexicographic ordering.

Requirement.Name: Coordinate Frame for Map
Requirement.Content: The system shall define a fixed map coordinate frame with the origin at the robot's start position and shall consistently map robot pose estimates to grid cell indices in this frame using the configured grid cell resolution.

Requirement.Name: Robot Pose Tracking
Requirement.Content: The system shall maintain an estimate of the robot's current pose, including position and orientation, in the map frame by integrating odometry and other relevant sensor inputs.

Requirement.Name: Map Update From Sensor Data
Requirement.Content: The system shall update the grid cell classifications based on incoming range or contact sensor data by projecting sensor measurements into the map frame using the current robot pose.

Requirement.Name: Cell Classification States
Requirement.Content: The system shall support at least three cell states: FREE for traversable space, BLOCKED for walls or obstacles, and UNKNOWN for cells that have not yet been observed.

Requirement.Name: Classification From Wall Detection
Requirement.Content: When a wall is detected along a sensor beam at a given distance, the system shall classify the corresponding grid cells up to the wall as FREE and the cell at the wall location as BLOCKED.

Requirement.Name: Unknown Cell Initialization
Requirement.Content: Upon system initialization, the system shall set the classification state of all cells within the configured maze bounds to UNKNOWN.

Requirement.Name: Explored Cell Determination
Requirement.Content: The system shall treat any cell whose classification has changed from UNKNOWN to either FREE or BLOCKED as explored for the purpose of exploration progress tracking.

Requirement.Name: Mapping Time Budget
Requirement.Content: The system shall enforce a maximum elapsed time of 5 minutes for mapping the maze, measured from the exploration start time defined as the transition from IDLE to EXPLORE until the instant the timeout condition is detected and mapping activity is terminated.

Requirement.Name: Time Tracking Mechanism
Requirement.Content: The system shall track the elapsed mapping time using a monotonic system clock with a resolution of at most 10 milliseconds.

Requirement.Name: Graceful Timeout Handling
Requirement.Content: When the 5-minute mapping time budget is exceeded, the system shall, at the instant the timeout condition is detected, stop exploring, terminate all further mapping updates, preserve the current internal map, and transition to the RETURN_TO_START state without disabling collision avoidance.

Requirement.Name: Clock Accuracy Over Mapping Period
Requirement.Content: The system clock used for mapping time measurement shall have a maximum drift of no more than plus or minus 1 second over a continuous period of 5 minutes relative to real time.

Requirement.Name: Grid-Based Path Planner
Requirement.Content: The system shall compute paths on the internal grid representation using only cells classified as FREE and shall treat BLOCKED cells as non-traversable.

Requirement.Name: Path Optimality Criterion
Requirement.Content: The system shall define path optimality as minimizing the total path length measured as the sum of Manhattan distances between successive grid cells along the path.

Requirement.Name: Shortest Path Algorithm
Requirement.Content: The system shall use a complete shortest-path search algorithm, such as A* or Dijkstra's Algorithm, to compute an optimal path on the grid from a specified start cell to a specified target cell according to the defined reachability and optimality criteria and the defined tie-breaking strategy.

Requirement.Name: Handling Unreachable Targets
Requirement.Content: If the planner cannot find a collision-free path to the requested target cell using the defined reachability criteria, the system shall report the target as unreachable and trigger selection of an alternative reachable target or terminate exploration based on the current state.

Requirement.Name: Path Replanning on Map Updates
Requirement.Content: When new map information changes the classification of any cell on the current planned path to BLOCKED, the system shall trigger replanning of the path to the current target from the robot's current position.

Requirement.Name: Reachable Cell Definition
Requirement.Content: The system shall define a grid cell as reachable from a start cell if there exists a sequence of moves between 4-connected neighboring cells, where each intermediate cell is classified as FREE, and shall treat cells that require traversing BLOCKED cells as unreachable.

Requirement.Name: Deterministic Path Tie-Breaking
Requirement.Content: When multiple shortest paths with identical optimal path length exist between a start cell and a target cell, the system shall select the path whose sequence of grid cell coordinates is lexicographically minimal when ordered by row index first and then column index, using the defined grid indexing convention.

Requirement.Name: Start Pose Recording
Requirement.Content: At system initialization, the system shall record the robot's start pose in the map frame and use it as the return-to-start target position.

Requirement.Name: Return Path Planning
Requirement.Content: When exploration is marked as done, the system shall compute a path from the robot's current pose to the recorded start pose using the internal maze representation.

Requirement.Name: Return Path Execution
Requirement.Content: The system shall command the actuators to follow the computed path back to the start pose while continuing to apply wall detection and collision avoidance, and shall monitor the path execution and emit PATH_COMPLETED, PATH_ABORTED, or PATH_TIMEOUT status signals according to the defined completion, abort, and timeout conditions.

Requirement.Name: Generic Path Execution Completion Condition
Requirement.Content: For any path execution, including RETURN_TO_START and SECOND_RUN, the system shall declare the path successfully completed by emitting a PATH_COMPLETED status when the robot's pose is within the configured position and orientation tolerances of the final target pose and no PATH_ABORTED status or PATH_TIMEOUT status has been or is being raised.

Requirement.Name: Generic Path Execution Abort Condition
Requirement.Content: For any path execution, including RETURN_TO_START and SECOND_RUN, the system shall declare the path aborted by emitting a PATH_ABORTED status when either a severe fault or safe-stop condition is signaled by the fault signaling mechanism or the defined forward progress criterion is not met for longer than the configurable timeout duration, and in case PATH_COMPLETED and PATH_ABORTED would be raised for the same path, the system shall treat PATH_ABORTED as dominant.

Requirement.Name: Path Execution Status Signaling
Requirement.Content: The system shall provide internal status signals PATH_COMPLETED, PATH_ABORTED, and PATH_TIMEOUT for each path execution and shall make these signals available to the exploration state machine so that transitions depending on completion, abort, or timeout of path execution can be evaluated.

Requirement.Name: Forward Progress Measurement for Abort
Requirement.Content: The system shall measure forward progress toward a path target as a decrease in a low-pass filtered Euclidean distance between the robot's current position and the target position in the map frame, and shall treat the robot as failing to make forward progress if this filtered distance does not decrease by at least 1 centimeter, allowing a hysteresis band of plus or minus 0.5 centimeters to avoid oscillations, over a continuous interval equal to the configurable timeout duration.

Requirement.Name: Path Execution Timeout Handling
Requirement.Content: For any initiated path execution, if neither PATH_COMPLETED nor PATH_ABORTED has been emitted within a maximum timeout duration equal to twice the configured forward progress timeout, the system shall emit a PATH_TIMEOUT status for that path and shall treat PATH_TIMEOUT as an abort condition equivalent to PATH_ABORTED for state machine transitions.

Requirement.Name: Second Run Initialization
Requirement.Content: After successfully reaching the start pose at the end of exploration, as defined by the PATH_COMPLETED status for the return path, the system shall transition from RETURN_TO_START to SECOND_RUN state without resetting the internal maze representation.

Requirement.Name: Target Selection for Second Run
Requirement.Content: At the beginning of the SECOND_RUN state, the system shall select the second-run target cell as defined by the configured target cell representation and shall verify that this cell is within the grid bounds.

Requirement.Name: Second Run Target Representation and Validation
Requirement.Content: The system shall represent the second-run maze target as a pair of grid coordinates in the internal maze grid and shall provide configuration parameters for these coordinates that are validated at startup to ensure they lie within the supported maze bounds and correspond to a cell that is FREE or UNKNOWN before the SECOND_RUN begins.

Requirement.Name: Efficient Path Computation for Second Run
Requirement.Content: Before starting motion in the SECOND_RUN state, the system shall compute the shortest path from the start pose to the selected target cell using the final internal maze representation created during exploration.

Requirement.Name: Second Run Path Execution
Requirement.Content: During the SECOND_RUN state, the system shall send actuator commands to follow the precomputed most efficient path to the target while still enforcing collision avoidance behavior and monitoring the path execution and emitting PATH_COMPLETED, PATH_ABORTED, or PATH_TIMEOUT status signals according to the defined completion, abort, and timeout conditions.

Requirement.Name: Second Run Completion
Requirement.Content: The system shall declare the second run complete when the robot pose is within the configured position and orientation tolerances of the target cell center in the map frame.

Requirement.Name: Path Completion Tolerances Configuration
Requirement.Content: The system shall define configuration parameters for position tolerance in centimeters and orientation tolerance in degrees, applied in the map frame, with allowed ranges of 0.5 to 10 centimeters for position tolerance and 1 to 30 degrees for orientation tolerance, to be used when evaluating completion of any path execution.

Requirement.Name: No Contact With Walls
Requirement.Content: The system shall select proximity and hard-stop thresholds and corresponding motion commands such that, under nominal sensor and actuator behavior, the robot chassis shall remain at least a configured minimum nonzero distance away from any wall.

Requirement.Name: Collision Avoidance Priority
Requirement.Content: In case of conflict between goal-directed motion and collision avoidance, the system shall prioritize collision avoidance and may temporarily deviate from the planned path.

Requirement.Name: Sensor Plausibility Checks
Requirement.Content: The system shall validate incoming sensor data using plausibility checks such as range limits, rate-of-change limits, and cross-sensor consistency before using the data for mapping, localization, or collision avoidance.

Requirement.Name: Faulty Sensor Data Handling
Requirement.Content: When sensor data is detected as faulty by plausibility checks, the system shall discard or down-weight the data and continue operating using remaining valid sensor inputs and the current internal state estimate.

Requirement.Name: Degraded Mode Operation
Requirement.Content: If one or more critical sensors are persistently faulty beyond a configurable duration, the system shall enter a degraded mode in which it reduces speed and restricts motion to previously mapped FREE cells only, and it shall continue to update the internal map for cells along the traversed paths while treating all UNKNOWN cells as non-traversable targets for path planning in degraded mode and allowing paths to include cells that become FREE as they are reclassified during motion.

Requirement.Name: Safe Stop on Severe Fault
Requirement.Content: When the system detects a severe sensor or actuator fault that prevents reliable collision avoidance, it shall command the actuators to stop motion and remain stationary until reset.

Requirement.Name: Critical Sensors and Fault Duration Configuration
Requirement.Content: The system shall define as critical all sensors whose sensor identifiers in the sensor interface are associated with wall distance measurements, obstacle detection, or odometry data, and shall provide a configuration parameter specifying the duration in seconds for which a critical sensor must be continuously faulty before degraded mode is entered, with an allowed range from 0.5 to 10 seconds.

Requirement.Name: Fault Signaling Mechanism
Requirement.Content: The system shall provide an internal signaling mechanism that emits fault events for severe sensor or actuator faults and hard-stop wall proximity conditions and shall make these events available to the actuator control subsystem for initiating emergency stop behavior.

Requirement.Name: Sensor Data Ingestion
Requirement.Content: The system shall receive sensor data messages as specified in the sensor data interface description and store them in an internal data structure with timestamps for subsequent processing.

Requirement.Name: Sensor Data Synchronization
Requirement.Content: The system shall synchronize data from multiple sensors based on their timestamps to produce coherent sensor frames for mapping, localization, and collision avoidance computations.

Requirement.Name: Sensor Processing Rate
Requirement.Content: The system shall process incoming sensor data at a nominal configurable rate within a range of 20 to 100 Hz, and each processing cycle shall start within plus or minus 5 milliseconds of its ideal periodic time to limit timing jitter.

Requirement.Name: Minimal Sensor Interface Properties
Requirement.Content: The sensor data interface shall provide, for each message, at minimum a sensor identifier, measurement value in standardized units, a timestamp, and a measurement validity flag so that the system can perform plausibility checks and data fusion.

Requirement.Name: Sensor and Actuator Update Rate Constraints
Requirement.Content: The system shall constrain periodic update rates for sensor processing and actuator command output to operate within a common configurable range of 20 to 100 Hz and shall ensure that the jitter of these periodic tasks does not exceed plus or minus 5 milliseconds from their nominal period.

Requirement.Name: Platform Performance Assumptions for Timing
Requirement.Content: The system shall assume a hardware platform that can execute all required sensing, mapping, planning, and control tasks at the configured update rates between 20 and 100 Hz with the specified jitter bounds, while maintaining average CPU utilization below 80 percent under nominal operating conditions, and these assumptions shall be used as constraints for architecture and platform selection.

Requirement.Name: Actuator Command Output
Requirement.Content: The system shall send motor control commands that conform to the motor interface description, including speed, direction, and duration or position parameters as required.

Requirement.Name: Motor Command Update Rate
Requirement.Content: The system shall update actuator commands at a nominal fixed rate within the configured 20 to 100 Hz range while the robot is in motion, respecting the jitter bounds defined for periodic update rates.

Requirement.Name: Velocity and Turning Control
Requirement.Content: The system shall convert planned linear and angular velocities into appropriate motor interface commands to achieve forward motion, backward motion, and in-place or curving turns as required by the navigation subsystem.

Requirement.Name: Emergency Stop Command
Requirement.Content: The system shall support an emergency stop command that immediately sets all motor speed commands to zero and shall issue this command when it receives a severe fault event or hard-stop wall proximity event from the fault signaling mechanism.

Requirement.Name: Minimal Motor Interface Properties
Requirement.Content: The motor interface shall support commands specifying linear velocity and angular velocity in standardized units, and shall guarantee that applied motor speeds achieve the commanded velocities within a response time of no more than 100 milliseconds after a new command is received at the configured control rate.
